sections.paragraph:
fut. יִמְכֹּר to sell (kindred to מָחַר, מָהַר, מוּר [“perh. Arab. مجر”]), Gen 37:27, 28 Gen 37:28 Lev 25:25 27:20 Joel 4:3 , etc.; followed by בְּ of price, Amo 2:6 Psa 44:13 specially-(a) to sell a daughter, i.e. to betroth her to any one (Syr. ܡܟܰܪ to betroth, compare מָהַר No. II.), Gen 31:15 Exo 21:7.-(b) to sell a people, used of Jehovah, i.e. to deliver into the power of an enemy, Deu 32:30 Jud. 2:14 , וַיִּמְכְּרֵם בְּיַד אֹיְבֵיהֶם “and he sold them into the power of their enemies;” Jud. 3:8 4:2 , Jdg 4:9 10:7 . Compare Nah 3:4.

Niphal נִמְכַּר.-

(1) pass. of Kal, to be sold, Levit. 25:34 pass. of letter b, Isa 50:1 52:3 .

(2) to sell oneself as a slave, Lev 25:39, 42 Lev 25:42, 47 Lev 25:47.

Hithpael-

(1) to be sold, Deu 28:68.

(2) to sell oneself, i.e. to give oneself up, to do evil, 1Ki 21:20, 25 1Ki 21:25 2Ki 17:17.

Derivatives, מָכִיר, מִמְכָּר, מִמְכֶּרֶת [pr.n. מִכְרִי], and-
